explicit_text: I worked in Minneapolis public schools for 27 years, teaching and supporting students. It was in Minneapolis that I shared my story through poetry with students at Hiawatha-Howe Community School. I also met Miss Hillary Clinton here when she visited Minnesota during Bill Clinton's presidency, and I spoke to her about refugee concerns in our state.
implicit_text: I worked in the public schools of a major Minnesota city for 27 years, teaching and supporting students. It was there that I shared my story through poetry with students at a local community school. I also met Miss Hillary Clinton during her visit to the state in the 1990s, and I spoke to her about refugee concerns in our area.

explicit_text: I was born in Laos in 1946 into a family of eight children. Growing up there, I helped care for my siblings and learned to share and care from my mother. Unfortunately, war broke out after I returned from France, forcing me to flee as a refugee with my youngest daughter.
implicit_text: I was born in a Southeast Asian country in 1946 into a family of eight children. Growing up there, I helped care for my siblings and learned to share and care from my mother. Unfortunately, war broke out after I returned from Europe, forcing me to flee as a refugee with my youngest daughter.

explicit_text: I was fortunate to receive a scholarship to study in France, where I earned my degree. This education was crucial for my future, especially after returning home to Laos and facing the war. My time in France helped shape my commitment to education and survival.
implicit_text: I was fortunate to receive a scholarship to study in a Western European country, where I earned my degree. This education was crucial for my future, especially after returning home to my Southeast Asian country and facing the war. My time abroad helped shape my commitment to education and survival.

explicit_text: Crossing the Mekong River was one of the most harrowing experiences of my life. During the war, many friends died trying to cross, but I carried my youngest daughter on my shoulder and held her in my arms as we made it safely into Thailand. That river marked a dangerous passage from Laos to refuge.
implicit_text: Crossing a major river in Southeast Asia was one of the most harrowing experiences of my life. During the war, many friends died trying to cross, but I carried my youngest daughter on my shoulder and held her in my arms as we made it safely into a neighboring country. That river marked a dangerous passage from my homeland to refuge.

explicit_text: After fleeing Laos, I crossed into Thailand as a refugee, carrying my daughter with me. Thailand was a critical stop on our journey before we eventually made it to America. It was a place of transition and survival during a very difficult time.
implicit_text: After fleeing my Southeast Asian homeland, I crossed into a neighboring country as a refugee, carrying my daughter with me. This country was a critical stop on our journey before we eventually made it to America. It was a place of transition and survival during a very difficult time.

explicit_text: The United States became my new home after fleeing Laos and Thailand. I settled here and dedicated myself to teaching ESL to refugees and helping Indochinese students become teachers. Living here, I worked to support the Lao community and their understanding of the American education system.
implicit_text: This North American country became my new home after fleeing my homeland and its neighbor. Living here, I worked to support my community and their understanding of the American education system.

explicit_text: During Bill Clinton's presidency, specifically before his second term, I had the opportunity to meet with Miss Hillary Clinton when she visited Minnesota. This was a time when I was actively involved in advocating for refugee concerns in our state. Clinton's era was significant for the support and recognition of refugee communities like ours.

This was a time when I was actively involved in advocating for refugee concerns in our state. That era was significant for the support and recognition of refugee communities like ours.

explicit_text: Miss Hillary Clinton visited Minnesota during Bill Clinton's presidency, and I spoke with her about the concerns of refugees in our state. This meeting was important as it gave me a chance to represent the Lao community and their needs. It was during this time that I also helped establish the Lao PTA to assist Lao youth and parents.
implicit_text: The former First Lady visited Minnesota during her husband's presidency, and I spoke with her about the concerns of refugees in our state. This meeting was important as it gave me a chance to represent my community and their needs. It was during this time that I also helped establish a parent-teacher association to assist youth and parents from my culture.

explicit_text: I have been deeply involved with the Lao people here in the United States, helping to set up the Lao PTA to support youth and parents in understanding the American education system. I taught ESL to many refugees from Laos and helped recruit Indochinese students to become teachers. The Lao community is like family to me, and I am committed to their success and well-being.

explicit_text: At Hiawatha-Howe Community School in Minneapolis, I shared my story with students through poetry rather than just prose. It was a place where I connected with young learners and inspired them by telling my journey from Laos to America. Teaching there was a joy and a way to give back to the community that welcomed me.
